items.title: Middle East: Trump again claims Iran peace deal is imminent
items.description: US President Donald Trump has said there was a "good chance" a peace deal in the Iran war could be reached in "two or three days." DW has the latest.

items.title: Israel kills 8 in southern Lebanon after issuing forced displacement order
items.description: Killings come as Iran warned of 'crushing blows' if Israeli attacks continued in Lebanon, including in the south.

items.title: Conflicts around the globe hit highest levels since World War II, new report says
items.description: A new report shows global conflicts surged in 2025, reaching levels not seen since World War II.

items.title: Ousted Senegal PM Ousmane Sonko re-elected party chief amid crisis
items.description: The speaker of Senegal's parliament, Ousmane Sonko, a powerful mentor-turned-rival to President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, won re-election Saturday as head of their party amid a political crisis sparked by their falling-out.

items.title: Armenia, once Russia's reliable ally, considers an EU future
items.description: Armenians are set to vote on the country's future geopolitical direction on June 7. The election will be a test of public opinion in the long-standing Russia ally, where the population is increasingly eyeing the EU.

items.title: News24 | Ceasefire in name only: Why powerful nations keep bombing and face no consequences
items.description: On Wednesday, Israel and Lebanon announced yet another ceasefire – after they had seemingly already agreed to a truce on 16 April.

items.title: News24 | IMF chief flags AI as potential world shock
items.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said that after facing crisis upon crisis in recent years, the world needs to build foundations that can withstand shocks that have become more frequent.

items.title: Thanks millions! New Zealand footballer meets Argentine influencer behind viral fame
items.description: New Zealand footballer Tim Payne meets online influencer Valen 'El Scarso' Scarsini. Leer más

items.title: Jailed crypto founder Sam Bankman-Fried seeks Trump pardon
items.description: The former leader of crypto platform FTX, currently serving a 25-year sentence, on Monday officially applied to be pardoned.

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Mexico promises peaceful tournament opening despite protests
items.description: A teachers’ union has threatened demonstrations at Thursday’s (June 11, 2026) opening game between Mexico and South Africa in the capital if the government doesn’t respond to demands for salary raises and pension reforms.

items.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas
items.description: A federal judge on Monday struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The administration announced the fee as a way of preventing foreign workers from taking American jobs.

items.title: Ousmane Sonko warns opponents after being elected PASTEF party chief
items.description: Sonko alluded to his split with Faye in a speech broadcast by the Pastef party.

items.title: Why Yad Vashem is coming to Germany
items.description: "Remember" — every child in Israel knows the meaning of this Jewish commandment: Never forget the Holocaust. Now Yad Vashem wants to make sure people in Germany remember it too.

items.title: Two charged over Minns donations but premier won't face legal action
items.description: Two men will be prosecuted over donations to Chris Minns's 2015 election campaign, but the NSW premier will not face any legal action himself.

items.title: More than 3,400 femicides in Argentina over past 11 years
items.description: Eleven years on from the first ‘Ni Una Menos’ march, 3,424 fatalities due to gender-based violence have been recorded in Argentina, according to tally kept by NGO. Leer más

items.title: US-China rivalry is laid bare by a contract to deepen an Argentine river
items.description: A government tender in Argentina underscored tensions between Washington and Beijing in Latin America as President Javier Milei’s administration awarded a 25-year contract to upgrade a key trade artery to a venture with a history of ties to China. Leer más

items.title: Indian diaspora groups welcome court ruling striking down $100,000 H-1B visa fee
items.description: A federal judge on Monday (June 8, 2026) struck down the $100,000 fee imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump for H-1B applications

items.title: It’s a small World (Cup)
items.description: FIFA has 211 member football associations, of whom only 80 have ever participated in a World Cup tournament in almost a century while for the highest level of winners, you would have to knock the zero off that figure – just eight countries, all of whom are past or present members of the European Union or Mercosur. Leer más

items.title: Argentina’s dollar buying tops US$10 billion goal on export boom
items.description: Argentina’s Central Bank has surpassed its US$10-billion target for dollar purchases, fuelled by a record crop harvest, an energy boom and a surge in debt issuance by provinces and local companies. Leer más

items.title: DR Congo court sentences 54 to death in final verdict on murder of UN experts
items.description: Nearly nine years after the killing of two United Nations experts in the Democratic Republic of Congo, the country's High Military Court has delivered its final verdict.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Reports: Milei plans to travels to US for Independence Day
items.description: Argentina’s President Javier MIlei intends to make his 18th visit to the United States to attend celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of American independence, according to reports. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Claws for concern: Bear sightings shut nearly 100 schools as hunters comb Japan streets
items.description: A bear roaming the streets of a Japanese city for three days forced the closure of nearly 100 schools, as dozens of hunters and officials searched for the animal.

items.title: Displaced migrants leave South Africa after xenophobic attacks
items.description: Displaced migrants following xenophobic violence in South Africa’s Western Cape province left the region by bus on Thursday.

items.title: UK reviewing Palantir's NHS contract amid pressure to use break clause
Injustice in justice
items.description: On the crying injustice of denying María Verónica Michelli a bench for which the government had nominated her on the grounds that her brother-in-law is a journalist probing cases of corruption affecting President Javier Milei’s administration. Leer más

items.title: New power minister promises visible improvement in electricity supply, says progress won’t be dramatic
items.description: “I am not promising a magic wand. I am not promising twenty-four hours, seven days a week power. We will get there ultimately,” the minister said. The post New power minister promises visible improvement in electricity supply, says progress won’t be dramatic appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: News24 | Norway’s crown princess faces lung transplant wait as royal family rallies by her side
items.description: Norway’s Crown Princess Mette-Marit, who suffers from an incurable lung disease, has been placed on a waiting list for a lung transplant.

items.title: Germany's Iran war lessons after 100 days
items.description: The war waged by the US and Israel against Iran has now lasted 100 days. In that time, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has drastically changed his stance on the war.

items.title: Nathaniel Bassey, Dunsin Oyekan, Yinka Akinselori to headline RCCG Mega Music Festival 2.0
items.description: The event is described as a national gathering of worship, prayer and prophetic declarations aimed at ushering in “a new season” for Nigeria. The post Nathaniel Bassey, Dunsin Oyekan, Yinka Akinselori to headline RCCG Mega Music Festival 2.0 appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Hunger in Oleshky: Ukraine asks for evacuation
items.description: The Ukrainian city of Oleshky has been occupied by Russian troops since 2022. Many residents are cut off from the outside world and living in dire conditions. Now, Kyiv wants to rescue them.

items.title: Friendship or leverage: Why is Xi Jinping in North Korea?
items.description: Beijing is trying to reassert influence over a strategically vital yet deeply unpredictable partner.

items.title: Singapore Airlines, Southwest Airlines partner to expand access to nearly 120 US destinations
items.description: Passengers can now book journeys between Singapore and the United States on one ticket with onward access to Southwest Airlines' network.

items.title: Schoolchildren run from collapsing roof as ground shakes in Philippines quake
items.description: Pupils could be heard screaming as the earthquake struck the city of Digos, but the school said nobody was injured in the incident.

items.title: India slams Pakistan at UN over 'Fitna al Hindustan' label
items.description: India lambasted Pakistan at the UN General Assembly over its anti-India rhetoric and its strikes on Afghanistan, which killed hundreds of civilians. Follow DW for more.

items.title: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
items.description: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

items.title: Scaloni says Argentina among ‘10 or 12’ teams challenging for World Cup title
items.description: National team coach lists 10 and 12 sides he thinks will be a threat at the 2026 World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Militants kill 6, abduct 8 security personnel in Pakistan: officials
items.description: The TTP posted an image of the men it had abducted on its social media channel and claimed responsibility for the attack
